paragraphs.en: Mark is a tourist in London. He wants to go to the train station, but he is lost. He asks a woman on the street for help.
paragraphs.fr: Mark est un touriste a Londres. Il veut aller a la gare, mais il est perdu. Il demande de l'aide a une femme dans la rue.

paragraphs.en: Excuse me, where is the train station, please?
paragraphs.fr: Excusez-moi, ou est la gare, s'il vous plait ?
paragraphs.speaker: Mark

paragraphs.en: The train station? It's not far from here. Go straight on this street.
paragraphs.fr: La gare ? Ce n'est pas loin d'ici. Allez tout droit dans cette rue.
paragraphs.speaker: Woman

paragraphs.en: Then turn left at the corner. You will see a big supermarket on the right.
paragraphs.fr: Ensuite, tournez a gauche au coin. Vous verrez un grand supermarche sur la droite.
paragraphs.speaker: Woman

paragraphs.en: Turn left at the corner. OK. Is the station near the supermarket?
paragraphs.fr: Tourner a gauche au coin. D'accord. La gare est-elle pres du supermarche ?
paragraphs.speaker: Mark

paragraphs.en: Yes, the station is just next to it. It's on the left side of the street. You can't miss it!
paragraphs.fr: Oui, la gare est juste a cote. Elle est sur le cote gauche de la rue. Vous ne pouvez pas la rater !
paragraphs.speaker: Woman

paragraphs.en: Is it far from here? How long does it take to walk there?
paragraphs.fr: C'est loin d'ici ? Combien de temps faut-il pour y aller a pied ?
paragraphs.speaker: Mark

paragraphs.en: No, it's not far at all. About five minutes on foot.
paragraphs.fr: Non, ce n'est pas loin du tout. Environ cinq minutes a pied.
paragraphs.speaker: Woman

paragraphs.en: Thank you very much! Have a nice day!
paragraphs.speaker: Mark

paragraphs.en: Mark follows the directions. He goes straight, turns left at the corner, and sees the supermarket on the right. The station is on the left, just like the woman said. He arrives in five minutes.
paragraphs.fr: Mark suit les indications. Il va tout droit, tourne a gauche au coin, et voit le supermarche sur la droite. La gare est sur la gauche, comme la femme l'a dit. Il arrive en cinq minutes.

vocabulary.definition_en: un endroit ou les trains ou les bus s'arretent
vocabulary.example: The train station is in the centre of the city.

vocabulary.definition_en: une route dans une ville avec des batiments de chaque cote
vocabulary.example: I live on a quiet street near the park.

vocabulary.definition_en: le cote oppose a la droite
vocabulary.example: Turn left at the traffic lights.

vocabulary.definition_en: le cote oppose a la gauche
vocabulary.example: The bank is on your right.

vocabulary.definition_en: en ligne droite, sans tourner ni devier
vocabulary.example: Go straight for two hundred metres.

vocabulary.definition_en: l'endroit ou deux rues se croisent
vocabulary.example: There is a cafe on the corner of the street.
The school is near my house.
The airport is very far from the city centre.

grammar_points.title: Imperatives for Giving Directions
grammar_points.explanation: Pour donner des directions, on utilise la forme **imperative**. C'est la base verbale sans sujet. Cela ressemble a un ordre, mais c'est poli quand on donne des directions. En francais, c'est comme l'imperatif avec 'vous'.
examples: Go straight on this street. (Allez tout droit.)
Turn left at the corner. (Tournez a gauche au coin.)
Walk for five minutes. (Marchez pendant cinq minutes.)
Don't turn right! (Ne tournez pas a droite !)

grammar_points.title: Prepositions of Place: on, next to, near, far from
grammar_points.explanation: On utilise des **prepositions** pour dire ou se trouvent les choses. **'On'** = sur (on the left, on the right). **'Next to'** = a cote de. **'Near'** = pres de. **'Far from'** = loin de.
examples: The station is on the left. (= sur la gauche)
The bank is next to the supermarket. (= a cote du)
The school is near my house. (= pres de)
Is it far from here? (= loin d'ici)

expressions.example: Excuse me, where is the post office?
expressions.note: La facon la plus courante de demander son chemin poliment en anglais. Commencez toujours par 'Excuse me'.

expressions.expression: It's on the left / on the right
expressions.meaning_fr: C'est sur la gauche / sur la droite
expressions.example: The pharmacy is on the right, after the bank.
expressions.note: S'utilise pour dire de quel cote de la rue se trouve un endroit.

expressions.example: Is it far from here? — No, just five minutes.
expressions.note: Une question simple pour demander la distance. On peut ajouter 'from here' pour plus de clarte.

written.question: Where does Mark need to turn?
written.explanation: The woman says 'Turn left at the corner.'

written.question: Where is the station?
options: On the right side of the street
On the left side of the street
written.explanation: The woman says 'It's on the left side of the street.'

written.question: How long does it take to walk to the station?
written.explanation: The woman says 'About five minutes on foot.'

written.sentence: ________ me, where is the train station, please?
written.hint: This is a polite word to start a question to a stranger.

written.sentence: Go ________ on this street and then turn ________ at the corner.
written.hint: These are two direction words from the dialogue.

written.french: Excusez-moi, la gare est-elle pres d'ici ? — Oui, allez tout droit et tournez a gauche.
written.suggested_answer: Excuse me, is the station near here? — Yes, go straight and turn left.

oral.instruction: Read the directions aloud clearly, as if you are helping a tourist.
oral.text: Go straight on this street. Then turn left at the corner. You will see a big supermarket on the right. The station is on the left, next to the supermarket.
oral.focus: Use a clear, helpful tone. Pause between each instruction. Stress the direction words: 'straight', 'left', 'right'.

oral.question: A tourist asks you: 'Excuse me, where is the nearest supermarket?' Give simple directions from where you are.
oral.suggested_answer: Go straight on this street. Turn right at the corner. The supermarket is on the left. It's not far — about three minutes on foot.

oral.question: Describe where your home is. Is it near a school, a shop, or a park? Use prepositions of place.
oral.suggested_answer: My house is on a quiet street. It is near a park and next to a small shop. The school is not far from my house — about ten minutes on foot.
